sanskrit: श्रोत्रं चक्षुः स्पर्शनं च रसनं घ्राणमेव च । अधिष्ठाय मनश्चायं विषयानुपसेवते ॥
hindiTranslation: यह जीवात्मा मनका आश्रय लेकर श्रोत्र, नेत्र, त्वचा, रसना और घ्राण  इन पाँचों इन्द्रियोंके द्वारा विषयोंका सेवन करता है ॥
englishTranslation: Taking hold of the mind, the inner being experiences objects through the ears, eyes, skin, tongue, and nose.
essence: Experience passes through mind and senses; it does not define the true self.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: The inner being uses the mind as a base and experiences the world through the five senses.
simpleInsight: What you call experience is filtered through mind and senses, not raw reality.
detailedMeaning: Krishna is explaining to Arjuna why the self seems tied to body and pleasure. After describing how the self moves from one body to another, he adds that it works through mind and the five senses. This verse supports the larger teaching that the true self is not the body.
modernRelevance: You scroll, taste, touch, and react, then call it 'me.' But the reaction is moving through your senses and mind, not defining what you are.
translationLiteral: श्रोत्रम् (ear) / चक्षुः (eye) / स्पर्शनम् (touch) / रसनम् (taste) / घ्राणम् (smell) / एव (indeed) / च (and) / अधिष्ठाय (having taken hold of) / मनः (mind) / च (and) / अयम् (this) / विषयान् (objects) / उपसेवते (enjoys, experiences)
